decision_points: IF nonconformance report received THEN create CorrectiveAction record with due date
IF recurrence rate > 5% THEN escalate to ImprovementProject
IF audit finding severity = critical THEN trigger immediate CAPA within 24 hours
rules: Every Nonconformity must have at least one CorrectiveAction assigned within 48 hours
CorrectiveAction must include root cause analysis before closure
All ImprovementProject must be linked to at least one KPI target
LessonsLearned must be reviewed before any QMS procedure update
exceptions: IF regulatory deadline conflicts with standard cycle time THEN override cycle time with documented justification
IF customer complaint involves safety issue THEN bypass normal triage and escalate directly to management review
data_requirements: root_cause: string (min 50 chars, required before closure)
corrective_action_due_date: datetime (calculated as detection_date + 30 days default)
triggers: performance gap detected via KPI threshold breach
success_criteria: corrective_action closed within target cycle time
recurrence rate reduced by >= 20% within 90 days
QMS effectiveness score increased or maintained
failure_modes: CorrectiveAction closed without verified root cause leading to recurrence
missing documentation of nonconformity causing audit nonconformance
KPI data not updated causing false improvement trend

capabilities: link lessons learned to QMS updates
enforce root cause and timeline rules
agent_spec.decision_logic: On trigger evaluate decision_points and rules; auto-create CAPA within 48h, escalate on recurrence >5% or critical severity, apply exceptions only with justification
escalation_rules: recurrence rate >5% or critical audit finding
CAPA closure attempted without root cause

sop:
# SOP — Improvement — Nonconformity and Continual Improvement
**Process ID:** ISO9001-10
**Framework:** ISO 9001:2015 | **Domain:** ISO 9001
**Generated:** 2026-06-12

## Purpose
Management of nonconformities and corrective actions, continual improvement of the QMS suitability adequacy and effectiveness

## Triggers
- new nonconformance report submitted
- audit finding published
- customer complaint logged
- performance gap detected via KPI threshold breach

## Inputs Required
- nonconformance reports
- audit findings
- customer complaints
- performance gaps
- improvement opportunities

## Process Steps
1. IF nonconformance report received THEN create CorrectiveAction record with due date
2. IF recurrence rate > 5% THEN escalate to ImprovementProject
3. IF audit finding severity = critical THEN trigger immediate CAPA within 24 hours

## Expected Outputs
- corrective actions
- improvement projects
- updated procedures
- lessons learned
- QMS improvements

## Business Rules
- Every Nonconformity must have at least one CorrectiveAction assigned within 48 hours
- CorrectiveAction must include root cause analysis before closure
- All ImprovementProject must be linked to at least one KPI target
- LessonsLearned must be reviewed before any QMS procedure update

## Exception Handling
- IF regulatory deadline conflicts with standard cycle time THEN override cycle time with documented justification
- IF customer complaint involves safety issue THEN bypass normal triage and escalate directly to management review

## Success Criteria
- corrective_action closed within target cycle time
- recurrence rate reduced by >= 20% within 90 days
- improvement project completion rate >= 85%
- QMS effectiveness score increased or maintained

## Compliance Requirements
- ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10
- CAPA requirements
- continual improvement mandate
test_cases: Test 1: happy path test: Agent receives a standard nonconformance report from production and successfully outputs corrective actions, updated procedures, and lessons learned that close the gap
Test 2: edge case: Agent is given a single vague improvement opportunity with minimal data and must still generate at least one valid QMS improvement output
Test 3: failure case: Agent receives malformed or empty audit findings and produces no outputs or incomplete corrective actions
Test 4: compliance check: Verify that every generated output (corrective actions, lessons learned, etc.) contains required fields and traceability references per ISO 9001 QMS rules
Test 5: escalation scenario: High-severity customer complaint triggers immediate escalation flag plus creation of an improvement project routed to senior management
deployment_notes: Agent ready for deployment. Compliance: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10, CAPA requirements, continual improvement mandate
